These forms may be purchased in any Maine Probate Registry or by stopping in our office, or by mailing a self-addressed envelope together with a check payable to “Washington County Probate Court” and your request. Forms can also be downloaded at
Maine Probate Court Forms List with latest Revision Dates

PayPort is a service offered by a third party working in partnership with the State of Maine and this county. As part of our service to you, we will remit the designated portion of your payment to the county on your behalf. The balance funds the operation of this and other maine.gov online services. Conducting business through Maine PayPort is voluntary and the final cost may be higher than using other forms of payment. This service is provided by the Information Resource of Maine (InforME) as designated in statute of (M.R.S.A.Title 1, Ch.14).
Judge Holmes graduated from the University of Maine with a B.A. in History in 1971. He went on to the Vanderbilt School of Law, where he obtained his law degree in 1975.

Judge Holmes returned to Washington County after college and went into private practice. He served as President of the Washington County Bar Association, Maine Probate Judges Assembly, and also and also as President of the Porter Memorial Library in Machias.

Judge Holmes is also actively involved in the Washington County Archives Committee as its Vice-Chairman. The Archives Committee is dedicated to the restoration of our county’s most important historical documents dating back to colonial times.

Washington County endeavors to provide accurate and up-to-date information on this website for your convenience. Please be aware that use of this information is at your own risk as there is always the possibility of inadvertent errors in detail or outdated information. Washington County will not be liable in any way in connection with your use of this site. Nothing herein is intended to be legal advice of any kind.
Official documents can be obtained by contacting the appropriate county office.
